<default aesthetic_system_instructions>
The user has not attached a design system. If they have ALSO not attached references or art direction, and the project is empty, you must ASK the user what visual aesthetic they want. Use the questions_v2 tool to ask about preferred vibe, audience, colors, type, mood, etc. Do NOT just pick your own visual aesthetic without getting the user's aesthetic input -- this is how you get slop!

Once answered, use this guidance when creating designs:
- Choose a type pairing from web-safe set or Google Fonts. Helvetica is a good choice. Avoid hard-to-read or overly stylized fonts. Use 1-3 fonts only.
- Foreground and background: choose a color tone (warm, cool, neutral, something in-between). Use subtly-toned whites and blacks; avoid saturations above 0.02 for whites.
- Accents: choose 0-2 additional accent colors using oklch. All accents should share same chroma and lightness; vary hue.
- NEVER write out an SVG yourself that's more complicated than a square, circle, diamond, etc.
- For imagery, never hand-draw SVGs; use subtly-striped SVG placeholders instead with monospace explainers for what should be dropped there (e.g. “product shot”)

CRITICAL: ignore default aesthetic entirely if given other aesthetic instructions like reference images, design systems or guidance, or if there are files in the project already.
</default aesthetic_system_instructions>

options: Redesign an existing storefront screen (e.g. homepage, product page, cart)

Modernize it — keep the structure, refresh the visuals

How many design directions do you want to see?

content.content:
Questions answered:
- goal: Redesign the seller / admin dashboard
- which_screen: Homepage, Login / register / onboarding, Seller dashboard, User account area, Cart & checkout, Product detail page, Category / product listing, Order tracking / logistics, Admin dashboard, Other, Decide for me
- fidelity: Bold redesign — new visual direction
- variations: 2–3 variations to compare
- explore_axis: Decide for me
- device: Both (responsive)
- market: Multi-language (show as English)

Got it — bold redesign of the seller/admin dashboard, 2–3 variations to compare, responsive, English. Let me ground the redesign in the *real* dashboard content (widgets, nav, metrics) rather than inventing generic ones.Now let me read the seller nav structure so my redesign has the real navigation.This is a complete, data-rich seller dashboard. I have everything I need: the real KPIs (Products, Rating, Total Orders, Total Sales), credit-score gauge, sales/profit chart, order-status counts, category breakdown, quick actions, top products, and the full sidebar IA. 

You delegated the aesthetic direction, so I'll build **3 bold, genuinely distinct directions** on a pannable canvas — each with a desktop dashboard + a mobile screen (your "responsive" ask):
- **A · Ledger** — editorial operator console, warm paper + ink, monospace figures
- **B · Signal** — dark command center, focused single accent
- **C · Market** — bright friendly retail, rounded and airy

Let me lay the foundation and build Direction A.Direction A is in. Let me check it renders correctly before building B and C.Direction A's ledger aesthetic is landing well — premium and clean. Now appending **Direction B · Signal** (dark command center) and tightening the mobile position.Now appending Direction B · Signal (dark command center).Now appending the third direction · Market (bright, friendly retail).All three directions are in. Let me view the full canvas to check layout, alignment, and that nothing overlaps.Preview is zoomed into Direction A. Let me capture a full overview to check the 3-column layout and verify nothing overlaps.All three directions render cleanly in a 3-column canvas — desktops on top, mobile screens below, no overlaps. Each is genuinely distinct in color, type, shape, and density while sharing the real data and navigation. Let me reset my preview zoom and hand off for verification.That console error is from a browser wallet extension (`chrome-extension://…/inpage.js`) scanning the page — not from the design. The dashboard itself renders cleanly (confirmed in the full-canvas overview). I'll finalize.Done. The only console message is from a browser wallet extension scanning the page — not from the design.

**Seller Dashboard Redesign** — 3 bold directions on one pannable canvas, each with a desktop dashboard + mobile screen, all driven by your real data and navigation (credit score, the 4 KPIs, sales/profit chart, order-status counts, category breakdown, quick actions, top products, grouped sidebar IA):

- **A · Ledger** — warm paper + ink, monospace figures, hairline density. Reads like a serious operator's console.
- **B · Signal** — dark command center, single lime accent, glowing hero revenue, conic credit ring.
- **C · Market** — bright and friendly, coral + rounded cards, large approachable type. Best for SMB sellers across markets.

Pan around the canvas to compare; drag the labels to rearrange. I improved the IA too — the flat 15-item sidebar is now grouped into **Sell / Grow / Finance / Account**.

Next steps when you've picked a direction: I can extend the winner across the other screens you flagged (login/onboarding, orders, product detail, cart/checkout, account, admin), add real interactions, and wire tweakable brand controls. Want me to drop the others and build one out?

contentBlocks.text: Got it — bold redesign of the seller/admin dashboard, 2–3 variations to compare, responsive, English. Let me ground the redesign in the *real* dashboard content (widgets, nav, metrics) rather than inventing generic ones.
